tagline: Concurrent HTTP load tester with live latency percentiles
author: Sola Samuel

epics.title: Concurrent Request Engine
epics.goal: Fire N requests across a worker pool using the fan-out/fan-in pattern and collect every result without data races.
epics.commit: #1 — feat: concurrent worker pool with fan-out/fan-in and percentiles

features.title: RequestSpec & CLI flag parsing
acceptanceCriteria: Missing --url exits non-zero with a usage message.
Repeatable --header flags accumulate into RequestSpec.Headers.
Defaults apply when flags are omitted.

features.title: Worker goroutine with directional channels
features.description: Implement worker() consuming jobs <-chan Job and emitting results chan<- Result; performs the HTTP call, measures latency, always closes the response body, defers wg.Done().
acceptanceCriteria: jobs is receive-only and results is send-only at the type level.
Response body is closed on every success path to allow connection reuse.
Worker exits cleanly when the jobs channel is closed and drained.

features.title: Coordinator fan-out / fan-in
features.description: run() starts N workers, feeds jobs in a separate goroutine that closes jobs, and a second goroutine that wg.Wait()s then closes results, while the main goroutine collects.
acceptanceCriteria: Exactly `total` jobs are fed and exactly `total` results are collected.
No goroutine leaks after run() returns (all workers exit, both channels close).
Buffered channels sized to concurrency.

features.title: Result collection & summary aggregation
features.description: collect() ranges over results, tallies totals/errors, classifies error kinds, and accumulates latencies into a Summary.
acceptanceCriteria: Summary.Total equals successes plus errors.
Error results contribute to ErrorKinds but not to Latencies.
Collection terminates when results is closed.

epics.title: Latency Statistics
epics.goal: Turn raw latencies into the percentiles, throughput, and error breakdown a reviewer cares about.
epics.commit: #1 — feat: concurrent worker pool with fan-out/fan-in and percentiles

features.title: Percentile calculation
features.description: percentile(sorted, p) returns the value at index (len-1)*p in the sorted slice; compute p50/p95/p99/max.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty input returns 0 rather than panicking.
p50/p95/p99 select the correct index on a known sorted slice.
max equals the last element of the sorted slice.

features.title: Throughput & summary formatting
features.description: Compute throughput as requests/elapsed and render the final summary: latency percentiles, throughput, and error breakdown by kind.
acceptanceCriteria: Throughput equals total successful requests divided by wall-clock elapsed.
Error breakdown lists each kind with its count.
Output matches the layout shown in the product overview.

epics.title: Live Terminal Experience
epics.goal: Show real-time progress and stats during a run without scrolling the terminal.
epics.commit: #2 — feat: live progress, error classification, duration mode

features.title: Atomic completion counter
features.description: A shared int64 incremented with atomic.AddInt64 by every worker after each request; read with atomic.LoadInt64.
acceptanceCriteria: Counter is updated lock-free with sync/atomic.
Final counter value equals total requests under -race.
No mutex is used for the counter.

features.title: Carriage-return progress bar
features.description: A progress goroutine on a 100ms ticker reads the atomic counter and overwrites the current line with \r, drawing a bar and done/total.
acceptanceCriteria: progressBar(done, total) renders proportional fill and clamps at 100%.
Display updates on the same terminal line (no scroll).
Progress goroutine stops cleanly when the run ends.

epics.title: Resilience & Run Modes
epics.goal: Classify failures precisely and support duration-based runs in addition to fixed request counts.
epics.commit: #2 — feat: live progress, error classification, duration mode

features.title: Error classification
features.description: classifyError() maps errors to kinds using errors.Is(context.DeadlineExceeded), errors.As(net.Error)+Timeout(), and a connection-refused check, defaulting to "other".
acceptanceCriteria: context.DeadlineExceeded classifies as "timeout".
A net.Error whose Timeout() is true classifies as "timeout".
Connection-refused classifies as "refused"; unknown errors as "other".

features.title: Per-request timeout via context
features.description: Each request is built with http.NewRequestWithContext and bounded by --timeout via the http.Client.Timeout and a cancelable context.
acceptanceCriteria: A slow endpoint exceeding --timeout produces a timeout-classified error.
The context is cancelled (defer cancel) when run() returns.

features.title: Duration mode
features.description: When --duration is set, feed jobs in a loop until a context timeout fires, then close jobs — running for time instead of a fixed count.
acceptanceCriteria: --duration and --requests are mutually understood; duration takes over feeding.
Feeding stops and jobs closes promptly when the duration elapses.
Summary reflects the actual number of requests completed in the window.

features.title: Connection pool tuning
features.description: Configure http.Transport.MaxIdleConnsPerHost = concurrency so keep-alive connections are reused across requests.
acceptanceCriteria: MaxIdleConnsPerHost equals the configured concurrency.
Response bodies are drained/closed so connections return to the pool.

epics.title: Distribution & Documentation
epics.goal: Ship blaze as an installable, well-documented tool that signals Go concurrency competence.
epics.commit: #2 — feat: live progress, error classification, duration mode

features.title: README with concurrency model
features.description: Usage examples, full flag reference, and an explanation of the fan-out/fan-in concurrency model with the diagram.
acceptanceCriteria: README documents every flag with its default.
README includes the fan-out/fan-in diagram and a comparison to hey/vegeta/k6.

features.title: go install support
features.description: Module path github.com/solasamuel/blaze with a main package installable via `go install ...@latest`.
acceptanceCriteria: go install github.com/solasamuel/blaze@latest produces a runnable `blaze` binary.
